promptbase_public_sources.observed_signal: PromptBase invites sellers to sell AI prompts or agent skills, including SKILL.md files.
promptbase_public_sources.local_implication: The route remains semantically strong for a Codex skill/template product, but any submission requires marketplace and account gates.

promptbase_public_sources.observed_signal: PromptBase support describes 20% marketplace fees, 0% referral-link prompt sales, 10% custom-job fees, and no extra hidden transaction fees in its examples.
promptbase_public_sources.local_implication: The local packet should model fee scenarios, but live payout route review remains blocked behind legal/payment/account approval.

promptbase_public_sources.observed_signal: PromptBase guidelines emphasize buyer value/use case and output consistency; prompts may be declined for lacking enough use case or being unstable/inconsistent.
promptbase_public_sources.local_implication: The local product needs proof that the skill kit has a clear buyer use case and reproducible local outputs before any submission.

promptbase_public_sources.observed_signal: PromptBase terms describe PromptBase acting as limited payment collection agent for sellers for marketplace payments.
promptbase_public_sources.local_implication: Legal/payment review must inspect seller obligations, payment collection, payout timing, and user responsibility before account or listing work.

promptbase_public_sources.observed_signal: PromptBase advertises a prompt marketplace with agent skills, free prompts, AI apps, custom creator hiring, and prompt stores.
promptbase_public_sources.local_implication: The listing route should be positioned as an agent-skill/template product, not as a guaranteed revenue product or unsupported marketplace claim.

ready_evidence.detail: The product manifest records 12 local Markdown/template files with SHA-256 hashes and no public listing readiness.
Existing package contains README, buyer guide, listing draft, screenshot plan, license/IP note, and templates.
PromptBase explicitly mentions selling agent skills/SKILL.md files; the product is a skill/template kit.
The digital-products hold register preserves marketplace demand, terms/fees, public listing, and account/payment setup holds.

blocking_gaps.reason: PromptBase submission guidelines need a route-specific mapping against the exact Agent Skill Starter Kit files after explicit read-only approval.
The route should prove clear use case and reproducible outputs locally before any marketplace-facing examples.
Fee, payout, payment collection, tax, KYC, refund, and seller obligations need human/legal/payment review.
No seller account, login, payout route, or agreement acceptance is approved.
No public listing, upload, price, product claim, or promotional action is approved.

local_checklist.instruction: Write a one-paragraph local use-case statement for AI builders who need reusable Codex skill scaffolding, without revenue or marketplace approval claims.
Map the sample SKILL.md and templates to reproducible local outcomes and expected files.
After read-only approval, compare current PromptBase submission guidelines to each product file and note required edits.
Prepare questions for marketplace fee, payout method, tax/KYC, refund, and seller-obligation review.
Prepare a do-not-run submission packet that lists every later action requiring explicit approval.

next_action: Create a local PromptBase use-case and reproducibility checklist from existing package files only; do not write final listing copy, browse marketplace pages in a lab browser, create accounts, accept terms, configure payouts, upload files, publish listings, mutate service requests, start workers, call model/MCP tools, or perform external actions.
